description: App-only / service account access to SharePoint via Microsoft Graph. No user sign-in required. The integration uses the application's own credentials and gets tenant-wide access. Best for centralized indexing, background sync, or shared workflows where every AI Hub user should see the same SharePoint content.

operations.name: Health Check
operations.description: Verify the connection — fetches a service token and confirms SharePoint is reachable.

operations.name: List Sites
operations.description: List the tenant root site plus any sites in the SharePoint search index. Newly-created sites have a 5-15 min indexing delay.

operations.name: Lookup Site by URL
operations.description: Find a SharePoint site directly by its URL (bypasses the search index — works for sites that haven't been indexed yet)

parameters.name: url
parameters.label: SharePoint Site URL
parameters.description: Full URL e.g. https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/MarketingTeam

operations.name: List Document Libraries
operations.description: List all document libraries (drives) in a SharePoint site

operations.name: List Files & Folders
operations.description: List files and folders in a document library or subfolder

parameters.name: type_filter
parameters.label: Item Type Filter
parameters.description: Restrict results to a single item type. Use 'Folders only' to iterate over subfolders, or 'Files only' to iterate over files. Useful when a folder contains a mix of both and the workflow only cares about one kind.

operations.name: Get File Info
operations.description: Get metadata for a specific file or folder

operations.name: Download File
operations.description: Download a file from SharePoint, preserving the original filename

parameters.name: dest_folder
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Absolute path (e.g. 'C:\temp\downloads') OR a subfolder under /uploads (e.g. 'reports/2026'). Leave blank to use /uploads root.

operations.name: Search Files
operations.description: Search for files in a document library

operations.name: List Folder (by Path)
operations.description: List items in a folder by path — workflow-friendly alternative to list_items. Supports filtering by name pattern and modified date for 'new files since last run' workflows.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Folder Path
parameters.description: Relative to drive root, e.g. 'Shared Documents/Inbox' or 'Reports/2026/Q3'

parameters.name: file_pattern
parameters.label: File Pattern
parameters.description: Optional glob filter (e.g. '*.pdf', 'Report_*.xlsx')

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.label: Modified After
parameters.description: Only return files modified after this ISO date/datetime (e.g. '2026-05-12' or '2026-05-12T08:00:00Z'). For workflows: pass last run timestamp.

parameters.name: type_filter
parameters.label: Item Type Filter
parameters.description: Restrict results to a single item type. Applied after file_pattern.

operations.name: Download File (by Path)
operations.description: Download a single file by its path. Preserves the original filename. Workflow-friendly for known file paths or when filename comes from a previous node.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Relative to drive root, e.g. 'Reports/Q3.pdf'

parameters.name: dest_folder
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Absolute path OR a subfolder under /uploads. Leave blank to use /uploads root.

operations.name: Download All Files in Folder
operations.description: Batch-download all files (matching optional name pattern and/or modified date) from a folder. Files keep their original names. Workflow use: 'process every new PDF in /Inbox since yesterday'.

parameters.name: file_pattern
parameters.label: File Pattern
parameters.description: Optional glob filter (e.g. '*.pdf')

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.label: Modified After
parameters.description: ISO date/datetime — only download files modified after this time (e.g. '2026-05-12' or last run timestamp)

parameters.name: max_files
parameters.label: Max Files
parameters.description: Cap on number of files to download (1-500)

parameters.name: dest_folder
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Absolute path (e.g. 'C:\temp\downloads') OR a subfolder under /uploads (e.g. 'reports/2026'). Created automatically. Leave blank to use /uploads root.

parameters.name: conflict_behavior
parameters.label: If File Exists
parameters.description: What to do when a file with the same name already exists at the destination

operations.name: Upload File to Folder
operations.description: Upload a local file to a SharePoint folder. Requires 'Files.ReadWrite.All' Application permission. Handles large files automatically (chunked uploads above 4MB). Specify the destination using EITHER Parent Folder ID (recommended for workflows iterating by id) OR Destination Folder Path. If both are provided, Parent Folder ID wins.

parameters.name: parent_item_id
parameters.label: Parent Folder ID
parameters.description: SharePoint item_id of the destination folder. Useful when the destination comes from a previous list operation — pass a variable holding the folder's id. Leave blank to use Destination Folder Path instead.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Destination Folder Path
parameters.description: Drive-relative folder path, e.g. 'Shared Documents/Reports'. Required only if Parent Folder ID is blank.

parameters.name: source_file_path
parameters.label: Local File Path
parameters.description: Local server path. From a workflow, pass a variable like '${generated_report}'

parameters.name: filename
parameters.label: Destination Filename
parameters.description: Override the filename in SharePoint (defaults to local filename)

operations.name: Upload Content as File
operations.description: Upload string or JSON content as a file. For workflows that generate text/JSON output and want to save it to SharePoint without writing to disk first.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.label: Filename
parameters.description: e.g. 'output.json' or 'report.txt'

parameters.name: content
parameters.label: File Content
parameters.description: Text or JSON. Use {variable} from a previous workflow node.

operations.name: Delete File
operations.description: Delete a file from SharePoint, by either item_id OR file_path. Workflow use case: clean up source files after processing. Requires 'Files.ReadWrite.All'.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id, e.g. 'Reports/Q3.pdf'

operations.name: Create Folder
operations.description: Create a folder (and any missing parent folders) in a document library. Useful for auto-creating output folders during a workflow run. Requires 'Files.ReadWrite.All'.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Folder Path
parameters.description: Path to create, e.g. 'CustomerA/Processed/2026-Q2'. Parent folders are created automatically.

operations.name: Move File
operations.description: Move a file to a different folder within the same drive. Workflow use: move processed files from Inbox to Processed. Optionally rename during the move. Requires 'Files.ReadWrite.All'.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id, e.g. 'Inbox/report.pdf'

parameters.name: dest_folder_path
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Target folder path, e.g. 'Processed/2026-Q2'

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.label: New Filename
parameters.description: Optionally rename the file during the move

operations.name: Copy File
operations.description: Copy a file to a different folder within the same drive. Original stays in place. Workflow use: archive originals before processing. Requires 'Files.ReadWrite.All'.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id, e.g. 'Inbox/report.pdf'

parameters.name: dest_folder_path
parameters.label: Destination Folder
parameters.description: Target folder path, e.g. 'Archive/2026'

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.label: New Filename
parameters.description: Optionally rename the copy

operations.name: Rename File
operations.description: Rename a file or folder in place. Workflow use: prefix processed files with 'PROCESSED_'. Requires 'Files.ReadWrite.All'.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.label: Item ID
parameters.description: Either item_id or file_path is required

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.label: File Path
parameters.description: Alternative to item_id

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.label: New Name
parameters.description: New filename including extension, e.g. 'PROCESSED_report.pdf'

operations.name: Import to Knowledge Base
operations.description: Download a SharePoint file and add it to an agent's knowledge base

operations.name: Bulk Import Folder to Knowledge Base
operations.description: Bulk version of Import to Knowledge — lists matching files in a folder (filtered by name pattern and/or modified date) and queues each one for background import. Returns immediately with the matched count. Per-file results appear in the Recent Activity log.

parameters.name: folder_path
parameters.label: Folder Path
parameters.description: e.g. 'Shared Documents/Inbox'

parameters.name: file_pattern
parameters.label: File Pattern
parameters.description: Glob filter (e.g. '*.pdf')

parameters.name: modified_after
parameters.label: Modified After
parameters.description: ISO date/datetime — only import files modified after this. For 'new files since last run' workflows.

parameters.name: max_files
parameters.label: Max Files
parameters.description: Cap on number of files (1-500)

parameters.name: description
parameters.label: Knowledge Description
parameters.description: Description applied to every imported document (defaults to the folder path)

setup_instructions:
## SharePoint Service Account Setup

This integration uses **app-only authentication** — no user sign-in. Best for centralized SharePoint access shared across all AI Hub users and for workflow automation.

### Step 1: Register Azure App
1. Azure Portal → [App Registrations](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps/ApplicationsListBlade) → **New registration**
2. Name: `AI Hub SharePoint Service`
3. Supported account types: **Accounts in this organizational directory only** (single-tenant is fine — app-only auth doesn't use /common/)
4. Redirect URI: leave blank (not needed for app-only auth)
5. Click **Register**

### Step 2: Configure APPLICATION Permissions (different from delegated)
1. **API permissions** → **Add a permission** → **Microsoft Graph**
2. Select **Application permissions** (NOT Delegated permissions)
3. Add the permissions you need:
   - `Sites.Read.All` — read sites (always required)
   - `Files.Read.All` — read files
   - `Files.ReadWrite.All` — required for upload, overwrite, and delete operations (workflow use)
   - `Sites.ReadWrite.All` — required if you want to write to specific site collections
4. Click **Grant admin consent** — required for all application permissions
5. Verify all rows show a green checkmark in the Status column

### Step 3: Create Client Secret
1. **Certificates & secrets** → **New client secret**
2. Copy the **Value** immediately

### Step 4: Find Your Tenant ID
On the app's Overview page, copy the **Directory (tenant) ID** — it's a GUID like `12345678-...`.

### Step 5: Connect in AI Hub
Click **Connect** below and paste the three values: tenant ID, application (client) ID, and client secret. AI Hub will fetch a service token and verify the connection on save — no user sign-in.

### Workflow Operations
This integration ships with both **item_id-based** ops (browsing UI) and **path-based** ops (workflow-friendly):
- `list_folder_by_path` / `download_file_by_path` / `download_folder` — read by path
- `upload_file` / `upload_content` — write files (handles 4MB+ via chunked upload sessions)
- `delete_file` — cleanup after processing

For workflow nodes, populate `drive_id` once (use `list_drives` to find it), then use path-based ops with dynamic filenames from previous node outputs.
